data.body: ADVISORY REMEDIATION (re-run round). Folded all valid findings: (CRITICAL, code-grounded) the 'sign over verified_at_sha' design contradicted signing.py — _canonical_payload signs only {v,algorithm,ticket_id,manifest} and head_sha is audit-only, so a new payload field would bump PAYLOAD_VERSION and INVALIDATE every prior signature. Remediated in S4: bind the SHA via the EXISTING signed MANIFEST CHANNEL (a 'verified-at-sha:<sha>' manifest step) — cryptographically bound + reproducible, zero payload/version change, prior signatures still verify; head_sha kept as audit metadata. Also folded: enumerate child IDs + de-risk ordering (S1 primitive first); source=local as documented back-out; ADR for the novel architecture; configurable tunables; per-ticket coverage/test ACs (cross-process flock test, periodic-reverify coverage, CONCURRENT byte-total TOCTOU test, value metric, ENOENT/unsigned test paths, pinned CLI<->MCP names, --source default, error-recovery targets, SHA-resolution fail-closed, head_sha disposition, claim-gate back-out). NOT auto-actioned: the generic deterministic 'AC-quality' heuristic (P6, non-specific); and the DECOMPOSITION concern (reviewer flags S2 [now 18 ACs] and S3 as epic-scale) — surfaced to user as a structural split decision, not silently split. Also: REBAR_LLM_MAX_TOKENS default doubled 8000->16000 (was the cause of S1's truncation; NOT overridden in rebar.toml).
